title: Muhammad's Marriage to Zaynab bt. Jabsh
description: woman, who captivated anyone who looked at her" (the words are 'A'ishah's). She had been captured during a raid on the Banu alMu^laliq and, in accordance with custom, became the slave of one of her captors. The latter agreed to free her in exchange for a sum of money. Juwayriyah approached Muhammad for help, and the latter, captivated by her beauty, offered her "something better" than payment of the price of her freedom-namely, marriage with himself. Other marriages during this period were to Rayhanah bt.

sources.citation: al-Tabari, The History of al-Tabari (SUNY), vol. 08
